scenario: Acme Insurance ships a Camunda 8.8 invoice approval process that handles 4500 invoices per day, with an approval timer, a rejection path, and an archive error path. The engineering lead wants a coherent automated testing strategy before promoting any new version of the BPMN model to production.
question: Which combination of test scopes do Camunda 8.8 best practices recommend for executable BPMN process definitions?

options.text: Run fast automated unit tests against a localized and isolated workflow engine, then validate releases with integration tests in an environment that closely mirrors the production setup
Run only integration tests in a shared staging cluster after every commit; unit-style tests against a localized workflow engine cannot exercise gateways, timers, or error events at all
Run only fast automated unit tests against a localized workflow engine and skip integration tests; a production-like environment is only needed when load testing the cluster
Run human-driven exploratory tests on the production cluster as the primary verification, and treat automated tests against a localized engine as an optional supplement before any release

a.text: Correct. The best-practice page tells you to test executable BPMN like any other software: write fast automated unit tests against a localized and isolated workflow engine and, before release, run integration tests in an environment that closely mirrors production, possibly including exploratory tests.

b.text: Incorrect. The best practice keeps fast unit-style tests against a localized engine as the foundation and only adds integration tests for the final pre-release verification, not the other way around.

c.text: Incorrect. Integration tests in a close-to-production environment are an explicit part of the recommendation before a release, not just a load-test concern.

d.text: Incorrect. Human-driven exploratory tests sit on top of automated unit and integration tests; they do not replace them as the primary verification layer.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 testing best practice layers fast automated unit-style tests on a localized engine with integration tests in a near-production environment, optionally including human-driven exploratory tests before a release.
